name: Cascada del Caozo Trail
name_original: Cascada del Caozo – running circular desde Valdastillas (PR-CC 32)
description: Jog the Cascada del Caozo Trail, a moderate 5.1-mile (8.3 km) circular route in Valle del Jerte, featuring the stunning Caozo Waterfall.
region_intro: Jogging around Valdastillas offers diverse terrain within the Jerte Valley, characterized by its cherry orchards, river basins, and the foothills of the Sierra de Tormantos. The region features varied landscapes, from gentle riverside paths along the Jerte River to more challenging ascents through chestnut and oak forests. Numerous waterfalls and gorges provide scenic backdrops and natural landmarks for running routes. Best jogging routes around Valdastillas The most popular jogging route is Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This route explores the area's impressive Nogaleas Waterfalls. Another top favourite among local runners is Cascada del Caozo Trail, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.3 km) path. This route leads to the notable Caozo Waterfall, offering scenic views. Local runners also love the La era de San Bernabé Trail, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through the Jerte Valley's diverse vegetation, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Valdastillas is defined by the Jerte Valley's cherry orchards, Sierra de Tormantos foothills, and numerous waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 runners have used komoot to explore Valdastillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Valdastillas?
A: Valdastillas offers a good selection of running routes, with over 10 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate paths to more challenging ascents through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Valdastillas?
A: The terrain around Valdastillas is quite varied. You'll find gentle riverside paths along the Jerte River, challenging ascents through chestnut and oak forests in the foothills of the Sierra de Tormantos, and trails winding through the famous cherry orchards of the Jerte Valley. Many routes also feature natural pools and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls in Valdastillas?
A: Yes, the region is renowned for its beautiful waterfalls. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991200556/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990926132/cascada-del-caozo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, leading to the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts the Marta Waterfall and the streams of the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve.

Q: Can I find family-friendly jogging paths in Valdastillas?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, many of the region's trails with low technical difficulty and shaded paths, such as sections of the Nogaleas Waterfalls itinerary, are suitable for families. These routes often feature natural pools and picnic areas, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in the Valdastillas area?
A: Generally, the natural trails and paths in the Jerte Valley are welcoming to dogs, provided they are kept under control. It's always advisable to check local regulations or signage at trailheads, especially within natural reserves like Garganta de los Infiernos, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What are some challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Valdastillas offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296805802/la-era-de-san-bernabe-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La era de San Bernabé Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 10 km path with significant elevation changes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991205414/penanegra-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñanegra Route&lt;/a&gt;, at nearly 18 km, provides a demanding run with rewarding views. The mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Tormantos and passes like Honduras Pass also offer strenuous options.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Valdastillas?
A: Yes, you can find circular running routes in the vicinity. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990929149/route-of-the-white-mojon-running-circular-from-piornal-pr-cc-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the White Mojón&lt;/a&gt;, a circular trail starting from Piornal, offering a good distance and varied scenery without needing to retrace your steps.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while jogging?
A: The running routes in Valdastillas are rich with natural beauty. You can expect to see the famous cherry orchards of the Jerte Valley, especially stunning in spring. Key landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the refreshing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pools of Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt;. Higher routes, such as those near Puerto de Piornal or Honduras Pass, offer spectacular panoramic views of the valley.

Q: When is the best time of year to go jogging in Valdastillas?
A: Spring is particularly beautiful for jogging in Valdastillas, as the Jerte Valley's cherry trees are in full blossom, creating a stunning white landscape. Summer offers refreshing natural pools and shaded paths, though it can be warm. Autumn brings vibrant colors to the forests, while winter, though cooler, can offer crisp, clear runs, especially at lower elevations.

Q: Is parking available near the running trailheads in Valdastillas?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in or near Valdastillas and at common starting points for popular trails. For specific routes, it's often best to check the route details on komoot, as many provide information on designated parking areas close to the trailhead.

Q: How can I reach the running trails in Valdastillas using public transport?
A: Valdastillas is accessible by public transport, typically via bus services connecting it to larger towns in the Cáceres province. While public transport can get you to the village, access to specific trailheads might require a short walk or local taxi service, depending on the route's starting point. Planning your journey in advance is recommended.

Q: What makes the Jerte Valley a unique place for running?
A: The Jerte Valley, with Valdastillas at its heart, offers a unique running experience due to its diverse and stunning natural landscape. It combines the iconic cherry orchards, the refreshing Jerte River, numerous waterfalls and natural pools, and the challenging foothills of the Sierra de Tormantos. This variety ensures scenic backdrops and varied terrain for runners of all levels.
tips: Undoubtedly one of the most beautiful and impressive waterfalls in the Piornal region, the Caozo waterfall is a wonderful place where nature can be appreciated at its best. It can be reached from Valdastillas following the marked route PR-CC 32.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Cascada del Caozo Trail?
faqs.answer: The circular jogging route, typically around 7 kilometers, takes approximately 1 hour and 3 minutes to complete at a steady pace. This estimate is for the full loop starting from Valdastillas.

faqs.question: Is the Cascada del Caozo Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 7-kilometer circular route is generally considered to be of low difficulty. While there's a small uphill section, much of the path levels out, making it suitable for those with varying fitness levels, including beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cascada del Caozo Trail?
faqs.answer: The route largely follows dirt roads, winding through orchards of cherry, olive, and chestnut trees. There are some sections with slight inclines, and parts might lead through an impressive oak grove. During the rainy season, the ground around the Caozo Waterfall can become quite humid and slippery.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and beautiful scenery, including views of the lush Jerte Valley, cherry, olive, and chestnut groves, and dense oak forests. The highlight is undoubtedly the stunning 30-meter Caozo Waterfall, which is particularly impressive after abundant rainfall. A metal footbridge allows for an up-close view.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Cascada del Caozo Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly beautiful during the cherry blossom season in spring, when the Jerte Valley is transformed into a 'white mantle'. However, this can also be a more crowded time. For the most impressive waterfall flow, visit after abundant rainfall in spring or autumn. Be aware that the ground can be slippery during rainy seasons.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Cascada del Caozo Trail?
faqs.answer: For the full circular jogging route, it's common to start from the town of Valdastillas. There is also a designated parking area very close to the Caozo Waterfall itself, from which a very short, 100-meter path leads directly to the falls.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Cascada del Caozo Trail?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, trails in this region are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near the waterfall, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or the Valle del Jerte?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access the Cascada del Caozo Trail or the surrounding Valle del Jerte. The natural areas are freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route follows the official 'Cascada del Caozo' trail for its entire length. It also briefly passes through sections of the '[CIMA CC02] Piornal * Valdastillas' trail.

faqs.question: Is the Cascada del Caozo Trail a loop or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The recommended jogging route is a circular loop, typically around 7 kilometers, often starting and ending in the town of Valdastillas. This allows you to experience diverse scenery without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for experiencing the Cascada del Caozo?
faqs.answer: While there's a very short, 100-meter path directly to the Caozo Waterfall from a nearby parking area, for a more rewarding experience, consider the longer 7-kilometer circular route starting from Valdastillas. This allows you to combine the waterfall visit with a scenic jog through the Jerte Valley's orchards and oak groves.
